title: Human Fracture Analysis Canonical ID Policy
purpose: Defines canonical naming, versioning, file identity, compatibility, and repository structure rules for the Human Fracture Analysis structural diagnostic system.

repository_logic.design_goal: Mirror the machine-readable GitHub structure used in STA so Human Fracture Analysis can evolve incrementally while preserving canonical references.

file_naming_rules: All file names use lowercase snake_case with explicit version suffix.
Canon files that bundle a range of IDs must include the ID span in the filename.
Signal registry and signal definitions are separate files.
Light files are allowed when only a first operational subset exists.
No file may silently overwrite a canonical object without a version bump.

interpretation.minor: Compatible extension, clarification, or added entries.

bump_logic: Bump minor when adding new cards, signals, test cases, or clarifications without breaking schema.
Bump major when field names, required structure, or semantic object logic changes in a breaking way.
Pinned versions spec defines which versions are currently active together.

immutability_rules: Canonical IDs are immutable once published.
Axiom statements may be clarified in a new version, but old IDs must remain traceable.
Deprecated objects must not be deleted without explicit deprecation metadata.
Test cases may evolve more freely, but version history should remain visible.

compatibility_rules.statement: Signal definitions version must be compatible with the signal registry version in active runtime.
Constraint engine must only reference existing signal IDs and canon IDs.
Response templates must only reference active assessment fields defined by the constraint engine.
Cards must bind only to valid signal IDs and canon IDs.
